title: Total nucleic acid extraction - NucleoMag® DNA/RNA Water Kit (Macherey-Nagel)
author: Adélaïde Roguet

steps_raw.instruction: For HA filter extraction, let the sample thaw on ice and go to step 2 . For BCoV/BRSV extraction (in duplicate), add 5 µL of BCoV/BRSV solution to the 2-mL tube containing 500 µL MWA1. Vortex for 15 seconds (speed 7 out of 10) and flash freeze the tube. Go to step 4. For Direct extraction , add 150 µL of wastewater to the 2-mL tube containing 100 µL CTAB. Vortex for 15 seconds (speed 7 out of 10) and flash freeze the tube. Go to step 4.
For HA filter extraction, place the 2-mL tubes in the bead beater. Mini-Beadbeater-16 (high-energy cell disrupter, BioSpec, 607, 1 speed, https://biospec.com/product/mini-beadbeater-16 )
Bead beat for 00:02:30 Start the bead beating when the beads start to be loose in the tubes.
Cooldown the samples on ice for 00:05:00 .
Repeat Steps 9.1 and 9.2 once go to step #2.1 .
Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 min at room temperature. 150000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute
For HA filter extraction, transfer 400 µL of supernatant to the Lysis/Bind plate . For BCoV/BRSV/Direct extraction , transfer all supernatant to the Lysis/Bind plate .
Start the protocol MN_96_Flex.bdz on the KingFisher Flex 01:14:00 Kingfisher Flex (Automated Extraction System, ThermoFisher, 5400630)

Transfer the purified sample from the Elution plate to the microcentrifuge tubes . The DNA/RNA is now ready for downstream applications. RNA extract may be stored in RNase-free water at -80ºC for 1 year.
Quantification by Droplet Digital PCR (ddPCR) dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bpg6mjze
